codeReview.summary: Fail. The fix does solve the multi-step transaction-history problem by persisting completed intermediate steps with Blockscout links, but it does not fully resolve the original post-write refresh failure. App.tsx now bumps refreshKey after confirmed writes, yet loadMarketSnapshot() still serves a 10-second cached snapshot keyed only by observer address, so a successful write can still leave both the market snapshot and recent activity stale until the cache expires. I did not find a new blocking regression in the transaction-history portion of the fix.

issues.description: The new onWriteSuccess callback in dualvm/src/App.tsx only retriggers loadMarketSnapshot(trackedAddress), but loadMarketSnapshot() immediately returns snapshotCache.value whenever the same observer address is requested within CACHE_TTL_MS (10 seconds). Because the fix never invalidates or bypasses that cache on write success, confirmed writes can still leave both the market snapshot and recent activity stale right after confirmation, so the original blocking refresh issue is not fully fixed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill is still too weak for refresh-related UX fixes: it tells workers to read src/lib/readModel/ loosely and to manually verify only that forms render/accept input, which allowed this fix worker to miss the concrete marketSnapshot cache behavior and ship another stale-read bug while still marking followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:28 and :50-54; the transcript skeleton for worker session 675e3bd9-3170-4190-8b09-aa052b947182 shows reads of App.tsx, WritePathSection.tsx, RecentActivitySection.tsx, TxStatusBanner.tsx, and useWriteFlow.ts, but no read of src/lib/readModel/marketSnapshot.ts and no browser wallet verification before commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not currently document that the frontend read layer keeps a 10-second in-memory snapshot cache keyed only by observer address, so UI-level refresh triggers do not force a fresh RPC read unless the caller also invalidates/busts that cache.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dualvm/src/lib/readModel/marketSnapshot.ts:9-27 implements CACHE_TTL_MS and early-returns snapshotCache.value, while /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md and /home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/environment.md discuss RPC limits/snapshot fallback but do not mention this local cache invalidation requirement.

summary: Fail. The fix successfully preserves visible per-step transaction history for multi-step flows, but post-write read refresh is still blocked by the read-model's 10-second cache, so market snapshot and recent activity do not reliably refresh immediately after successful writes. Shared-state follow-up: document the cache behavior and tighten the frontend-worker skill so refresh fixes must inspect cache-bearing read-model code and validate a connected write flow.
